step_by_step_reasoning: Aqua SaaS connects via NLB to EKS nodes in two Availability Zones. Each EKS node runs the customer application and an Aqua Agent daemonset for runtime security and process blocking.

graph.name: Aqua Security CNAP Protection on AWS EKS
graph.notes: Aqua Security's CNAP platform protects containerized workloads on EKS using a daemonset agent to monitor and block malicious kernel-level processes.

nodes.name: Aqua SaaS
nodes.notes: Aqua's central management plane running as a SaaS platform.

nodes.name: Network Load Balancer
nodes.notes: Routes traffic between the external Aqua SaaS and the internal EKS cluster nodes.

nodes.name: EKS Node (AZ 1)
nodes.notes: Runs customer application containers in AZ 1.

nodes.name: Aqua Agent (AZ 1)
nodes.notes: Daemonset agent monitoring processes and kernel behaviors on the AZ 1 EKS node.

nodes.name: EKS Node (AZ 2)
nodes.notes: Runs customer application containers in AZ 2.

nodes.name: Aqua Agent (AZ 2)
nodes.notes: Daemonset agent monitoring processes and kernel behaviors on the AZ 2 EKS node.

edges.notes: Routes management traffic to EKS node in AZ 1.
Routes management traffic to EKS node in AZ 2.
